channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: 1976 Fender Precision Bass – Natural Ash / Maple with OHSC — $2,900
item.description:
$2,900 · Very Good · Damon's Surplus Gear

Up for sale is a very nice 1976 Fender Precision Bass in classic original Natural finish on ash body with a one-piece maple “B” neck. This bass features all original electronics and finish. It weighs in at 9.5 lbs, and includes the original hard shell case along with the original manual and cleaning cloth. The only items missing are the chrome pickup ashtray cover and the original thumb rest. I installed an after market thumb rest. Overall condition of the bass is Very Good with only minor scratches, small dings, and light buckle rash consistent with age. The neck is straight and the truss rod turns easily and works as it should. The frets could probably use a level, crown, and polish job but they're in decent and playable shape as is. Just an all-around great-playing and great-sounding vintage P-Bass with excellent collector appeal. But this one deserves to be on a stage.

Feel free to ask questions. Thanks for looking!

item.title: EFB-1 - Sunburst ( Bluetooth+Speaker+Drum machine ) — $312
item.description:
$312 · Poor · Ant Guitar

﻿﻿

  • Body : Alder
  • Weight : 7 Lbs
  • Finish : Satin Polyester
  • Cutaway : Double cut
  • Neck : Maple
  • Neck Finish : Satin Urethane
  • Neck Shape : Modern "U"
  • Scale Length : 25.5" (648 mm)
  • Fingerboard : Blackwood
  • Frets : 24F
  • Nut Material : Synthetic Bone
  • Nut Width : 42.8mm)
  • Position Inlays : White Dots
  • Truss Rod : Standard
  • Truss Rod Nut : 1/8"
  • Bridge Pickup : 2 single coil, 1 double coil
  • Controls : Master Volume, Tone 1 (Neck & Middle Pickup), Tone Switching: 5-Position
  • Special Electronics : Bluetooth, Drum bit, Push / Pull Switch up on Tone 2 adds Neck Pickup in Parallel for Positions 1 & 2
  • Bridge : 6-Saddle Vintage-Style Synchronized Tremolo
  • Hardware Finish : Nickel / Chrome

Overall, it is clean and in good condition, with some minor cosmetic scratches from normal use.

It shows little sign of wear and remains fully playable, ready to use right away.

It is equipped with a built-in speaker featuring amp modeling functions, allowing you to play virtually anywhere without additional equipment. It also includes 40 built-in drum rhythms with adjustable tempo and volume settings, providing a more engaging and versatile practice experience.

In addition, a built-in Bluetooth receiver allows you to play along with your favorite music wirelessly.

As with any pre-owned item, there may be minor signs of use, but the overall condition is good. Please take this into consideration before purchasing.

item.title: Fender Player Jazz Bass with Maple Fretboard 2018 - 2024 - Buttercream — $550
item.description:
$550 · Very Good · Jay's Echo Chamber

Very cool J bass. Has a few superficial paint cracks. Comes with strap locks and hard shell case.

item.title: Fender Standard Jazz Bass with Rosewood Fretboard 2002 - 2005 - Sage Green Metallic — $600
item.description:
$600 · Very Good · Chris's Gear Garage

Will add decription later. Great guitar.

item.title: ESP Horizon Bass II 90s - Black — $1,325.36
item.description:
$1,325.36 · Good · Ryan's Gear Depot

Hi guys! I would like to sell my ESP Horizon bass from the 90s. (japan shop) It has some rather... interesting... decals on it, lol.

The previous owner claimed it came from the shop custom like that, it does seem like so!

It has some small dings in the body but is mostly in great condition.

The screws could use some replacing and cleaning up.

I just wanted to post it and see if I get any offers, thanks guys.

I would freaking adore to have the same Horizon in guitar form, if anyone wanted to arrange a trade.

Comes with an old very worn ESP (possibly off brand) case.


If you have any questions just let me know, thanks!

item.title: Fender Player II Jazz Bass - Gray - Maple Fingerboard - NEW ! — $849.99
item.description:
$849.99 · Brand New · FLORES MUSIC

Fender Player II Jazz Bass - Gray - Maple Fingerboard - NEW !


Brand New

Features:

  • Iconic Fender J Bass sound and playability with contemporary updates
  • Offset alder body ready to perform at gigs or jam sessions
  • Maple neck with Modern “C” carve plays fast and fluid
  • Maple fingerboard with 20 frets and 9.5-inch radius
  • Standard open-gear tuners and vintage-style disc string tree provide stylish functionality
  • Synthetic bone nut and 4-saddle standard bridge hold down the intonation
  • Player series Alnico V single-coil Jazz Bass pickups for sweet highs and seismic lows
  • Per-pickup volume knobs and master tone knob to sculpt your sound
  • Available in timeless finishes and unseen colors released from the vault


Tech Specs

  • Number of Strings:4
  • Left-/Right-handed:Right-handed
  • Body Shape:Player II Jazz Bass
  • Body Material:Alder
  • Color: Gray
  • Body Finish:Gloss Polyester
  • Neck Material:Maple
  • Neck Shape:Modern C
  • Neck Joint:Bolt-on
  • Radius:9.5"
  • Fingerboard Material:Maple
  • Fingerboard Inlay:Dots
  • Number of Frets:20
  • Scale Length:34"
  • Nut Width:1.5"
  • Nut Material:Synthetic Bone
  • Bridge/Tailpiece:4-Saddle Bridge with Single-Groove Steel "Barrel" Saddles
  • Tuners:Standard Open-Gear
  • Neck Pickup:Fender Player Series Alnico V Jazz Single-coil
  • Bridge Pickup:Fender Player Series Alnico V Jazz Single-coil
  • Controls:2 x volume, 1 x tone
  • Strings:Fender 7250M, .045-.105
  • Case/Gig Bag:Sold Separately

item.title: Fender American Professional II Precision Bass with Rosewood Fretboard 2020 - Present - Dark Night — $1,250
item.description:
$1,250 · Excellent · I Think We Have All Seen Bonanza

Excellent+ condition. Still has plastic on pickguard.

item.title: Fender Musicmaster Bass 1978-1979 - Wine Red — $1,395
item.description:
$1,395 · Very Good · Tower Bridge Music

Fender Musicmaster Bass – Made in USA – Late 1970s (Likely 1978–1979) – Original Red Finish

Here’s a chance to own a genuine vintage Fender Musicmaster Bass from the late 1970s. Based on the serial number (S839414), headstock logo style, hardware, and construction details, this bass was most likely produced in 1978 or 1979.

The Musicmaster Bass has become increasingly sought after thanks to its lightweight body, comfortable short-scale design, and surprisingly punchy single-pickup sound. Originally introduced as Fender’s student model, these have developed a strong following among players and collectors alike.

This example is in very nice vintage condition and has recently been cleaned and detailed. The frets have been polished, the rosewood fretboard has been cleaned and conditioned, and it is fitted with a fresh set of Fender 9050 flatwound strings, giving it the classic thumpy short-scale tone these basses are known for.

The original transparent red finish still presents beautifully and allows the wood grain to show through. There are a few minor scratches and signs of normal use consistent with a nearly 50-year-old instrument, all of which have been photographed as accurately as possible. Overall, it remains an exceptionally clean example for its age.

Features include:

• Made in USA
• Likely produced in 1978–1979
• Original transparent red finish
• Short-scale 30” scale length
• Rosewood fretboard
• Original single-coil pickup
• Original Fender tuners
• Original bridge and hardware
• Fresh Fender 9050 flatwound strings
• Frets polished
• Fretboard cleaned and conditioned
• Thoroughly cleaned and detailed

The neck is comfortable and easy to play, and the bass delivers the warm, focused tone that has made vintage Musicmaster Basses increasingly popular with studio musicians and collectors.

A fantastic opportunity to own a genuine vintage Fender at a fraction of the cost of a Precision or Jazz Bass from the same era.

item.title: Sterling StingRay Classic Ray24CA 2024 - Present - Olympic White — $299
item.description:
$299 · Good · Joe 's Shop

When taking pics to sell this bass I discovered very very small hairline crack in body .. the bass has been in my house inside of a heavy duty gator case .. I don’t know what caused this .. bass has never been dropped and I haven’t gigged out with it .. everything else is in perfectly fine working order .. many tone variations. I put a bass ramp on pick guard. Also the headstock collage are just skate stickers .. If you buy bass and want me to remove before shipping , I can do that .. thanks for looking .

item.title: Sandberg TT4 - Jazz bass Burst — $1,799.99
item.description:
$1,799.99 · Excellent · Will's Gear Outlet

Sandberg California TT 4 tobacco sunburst only mark on top of headstock and shown in photos. 9.5 out of 10. Comes with Aguilar HC pickups installed. Original pickups with be in the included gigbag and truss rod tool.

item.title: Warwick Rockbass Corvette $$ 4 2022 - Honey Violin Transparent — $950
item.description:
$950 · Excellent · Joe 's Shop

Bass is in great condition. I had a full set up on it in February. plays like butter . Active MEC $$ PIckups . You can see how clean the wiring is in the easy access electronics compartment. so Has Dunlop Strap locks, comes with Warwick gig bag. Many different tone variations with this bass. Thanks for looking.

item.title: Ibanez BTB605MS-CEM Bass Workshop 2022 - Present - Cerulean Aura Burst Matte — $969
item.description:
$969 · Excellent · Zachary's Gear Outlet

All original packaging and documentation included.

Instrument is in near perfect condition. Just some little marks on the pickups from playing. Barely able to capture them in photos. The case has some rub marks and stuff on it from travel but nothing major or concerning.

Will ship in the original Ibanez box from the factory.

item.title: Mike Lull Bryan Beller Signature (BBMF5) — $6,000
item.description:
$6,000 · Excellent · Rocketweezie Sales

Recently purchased pre-owned from Mike Lull for a specific project. The project has fallen through, and this insane bass needs to be appreciated by the masses on stage. Its tone is huge, growling, and articulate. Playability is second to none. There are some very, very light surface scratches on the back from previous use. They are barely noticeable. Spencer posted a video of this bass on the Lull FB page.

item.title: Bronson Left Hand Mustang 2026 - Cherry Stain satin Finish — $1,350
item.description:
$1,350 · Brand New · Bronson Guitar and Bass Shop

just finished out of my build shop,

Bronson Left Hand short scale Mustang style Bass,

Alder body stained semi transparent cherry, thru body bridge, Maple neck with rosewood fret board, 38mm bone nut, Nordstrand NM4 pickup set.

item.title: SQUIER - CLASSIC VIBE 60S JAZZ BASS — $453.86
item.description:
$453.86 · Brand New · Hurricane Music

Basse Squier Classic Vibe™ '60s Jazz Bass® – Tahitian Coral

La Classic Vibe™ '60s Jazz Bass® est un hommage vibrant aux modèles légendaires des années 1960. Elle reprend l’esthétique et les caractéristiques sonores de cette décennie iconique tout en offrant une jouabilité moderne, fidèle à l’héritage Fender. Avec son coloris Tahitian Coral et son pickguard écaille 4 plis, elle se démarque visuellement et séduira les amateurs de vintage au look affirmé.

Une esthétique 60’s authentique

Dotée d’un corps en peuplier au vernis polyuréthane brillant, cette Jazz Bass affiche un look rétro assumé. Le manche érable teinté vintage, associé à une touche en laurier indien avec incrustations pearloid et repères latéraux blancs, renforce encore cet esprit sixties tout en garantissant une prise en main confortable.

Des micros conçus par Fender

Elle embarque deux micros simples Alnico Fender Designed™ (manche et chevalet), offrant une réponse dynamique, un grain chaleureux et une grande polyvalence. Ce système en configuration SS permet de passer de lignes de basse rondes et douces à des attaques plus tranchantes, parfaites pour le funk, le rock ou la soul.

Confort et précision de jeu

Le profil de manche en "C" combiné au rayon de touche de 9,5" et aux frettes Narrow Tall favorise un jeu fluide, tant en accompagnement qu’en solo. Le chevalet vintage à 4 pontets filetés assure une intonation stable, un bon sustain et une esthétique fidèle à l’original.

Caractéristiques techniques
  • Modèle : Classic Vibe™ '60s Jazz Bass®
  • Référence : 0374530511
  • Couleur : Tahitian Coral
  • Orientation : Droitier
  • Corps : Peuplier
  • Finition : Gloss Polyurethane
  • Manche : Érable, profil "C", finition teintée vintage brillante
  • Touche : Indian Laurel, radius 9.5", 20 frettes Narrow Tall
  • Sillet : Os véritable, largeur 38.1 mm
  • Diapason : 34" (864 mm)
  • Micros : 2 x Fender Designed™ Alnico Single-Coil
  • Réglages : Volume manche, Volume chevalet, Tonalité générale
  • Chevalet : Vintage-style à 4 pontets filetés
  • Accastillage : Nickelé
  • Pickguard : 4 plis écaille
  • Poids expédition : 11,50 lb
Un classique revisité pour les musiciens d’aujourd’hui

La Classic Vibe™ '60s Jazz Bass® est une basse au caractère vintage affirmé, qui combine look rétro, sonorité Fender authentique et ergonomie moderne. Que vous soyez amateur de lignes de basse rondes ou de grooves incisifs, elle saura répondre à toutes vos attentes avec style et efficacité.

item.title: STERLING BY MUSIC MAN - STINGRAY CLASSIC RAY24CA OLYMPIC WHITE — $648.52
item.description:
$648.52 · Brand New · Hurricane Music

Découvrez une basse qui allie performance et style

La StingRay Classic RAY24CA Olympic White de Sterling by Music Man est le choix idéal pour les musiciens à la recherche d'un instrument alliant confort et son exceptionnel. Offrant une esthétique élégante avec son coloris Olympic White, cette basse est conçue pour répondre à toutes vos attentes musicales.

Caractéristiques
  • Accastillage : Chromé
  • Catégorie : Basse 4-cordes
  • Chevalet/cordier : Sterling
  • Coloris : Olympic White
  • Cordes d'origine : Ernie Ball 2834 Super Slinky
  • Corps : Nyatoh
  • Diapason : 864 mm
  • Électronique : Active
  • Finition : Vernis brillant
  • Format : Stingray
  • Largeur au sillet : 38 mm
  • Manche : Érable
  • Mécaniques : Ouvertes
  • Micro chevalet : Ceramic Humbucker
  • Nombre de cordes : 4
  • Nombre de frettes : 21
  • Nombre de micros : 1
  • Radius : 240 mm
  • Réglages : Égalisation 2-bandes, 1 x Volume
  • Touche : Érable
  • Type de frette : Medium Jumbo

item.title: FENDER - PLAYER II PRECISION BASS RW POLAR WHITE — $840.19
item.description:
$840.19 · Brand New · Hurricane Music

  • Corps : Alder
  • Finition du corps : Gloss Polyester
  • Forme du corps : Precision Bass®
  • Boutons de commande : Knurled Flat-Top

  • Repères Latéraux : Blancs
  • Tête de Manche : Precision Bass®
  • Orientation : Right-Hand

  • Type des Micros : Single Coil

  • Type de Radius de Touche : Modern
  • Taille des frettes : Medium Jumbo
  • Matériau du manche : Maple
  • Type de Matériau du Manche : Maple
  • Forme du manche : Modern "C"
  • Type de Profil du Manche : C Shape
  • Nombre de frettes : 20
  • Matériau du Sillet de Tête : Synthetic Bone
  • Largeur de sillet : 1.625" (41.3 mm)
  • Longueur de l'échelle : 34" (86.36 cm)
  • Tige de serrage : Single Action, Head Adjust
  • Écrou de la Tige de Réglage du Manche : 3/16" Hex Adjustment
  • Style : 2 Single-Coil

item.title: TAKAMINE - BASSE GB30CE — $463.80
item.description:
$463.80 · Brand New · Hurricane Music

Cela fait plus de 50 ans que les luthiers de Takamine, travaillent avec les artistes du monde entier. Grâce à toutes ces rencontres et à leur expérience longue de plus d'un demi-siècle, ils ont pu façonner une nouvelle série de basses d'une qualité de fabrication époustouflante!

-Table : Epicéa Massif
-Dos & Eclisses : Acajou
-Corps : Vernis Brillant
-Manche : Acajou
-Touche : Palissandre
-Sillet de tête : 45mm
-Sillet de chevalet : Os Synthétique
-Electronique : TK-40B

item.title: Fender Standard Precision Bass — $549
item.description:
$549 · Very Good · Blue Smokies Music Gear

Just typical minor scratches from use. no cracks or damage. Just recently had it setup with brand new strings. Ready to plug and play.

item.title: Woodcraft electric guitars AmpMaster 4 Bass short scale 30" Vintage White — $1,349.99
item.description:
$1,349.99 · Brand New · Amp Shop Bass Exchange

Woodcraft Electric Guitars AmpMaster 4 Bass 30"  Vintage White 4 string Bass 30" Short Scale,

super light

Alder Vintage White Body, Hard Rock Maple Neck with Wenge Fingerboard 

"Easy-Wheel" Dual Action Truss Rod and 2 pcs of Double Titanium Reinforcement Rods. All Woodcraft basses utilize Dual Truss Rods “Easy Wheel” truss rod adjustment system made from Titanium to maintain neck stiffness, stability and reduce weight. Easy Wheel allows you to make the adjustment on the spot without special wrenches.

There are a lot of basses on the market with a tendency to have weak neck binding structures due to high string tensions. For this reason, two Titanium rods are applied to all bass guitars.

TUSQ NUT: The nut is one of the most important parts of the bass that influences the performance, playability, and tone of the instrument. Depending on the quality of the material, the nut can either improve or degrade the sound. Although the TUSQ nut is more costly and harder to work with, it is considered to deliver more balanced, clearer and smoother tones than brass, corian or other materials.

ACCURATE AND STABLE TUNING: This bass comes with GOTOH/Albridge  light weight tuners. Developed with accurate gear ratios, the machine heads are very reliable in tuning, Albridge

Albridge parts hand wound Super 8 Split Coil pickup, large poled noise free, NO 60 cycle Hum.

Custom wiring CTS pots with disk filter capacitor, graphite cavity shielded

Neck plate 6 holes Duralumin, light

BRIDGE: This bass comes with an ALBRIDGE parts Custom Light Weight Duralumin bridge, Air space grade with Bell Bronze saddles and extra-hard tension saddle springs to eliminate annoying parasitic rattles/vibrations coming from other types of bridges. String space 18mm.


Ampmaster neck: Nut size 38.1mm, at 20th fret 60mm

Width under 1st fret 20mm, 12th fret 22.5mm

Fretboard Radius 12”

Fret’s width 2.8mm (0.11”)

Bass Overall length is 40 3/4 in. (1035 mm.), 12 1/4 in. (31.1 cm.) wide at lower bout, 

and 1 1/2 in. (3.8 cm.) in depth, measured at the side of the rim. Scale length is 30 in. (762 mm.).


Thank you for buying Woodcraft Electric Guitar product, North Hollywood, California U.S.A.  ASBX

item.title: Fender JAB J-Craft Jaguar Bass 2005 - 2014 - Red — $700
item.description:
$700 · Good · THUNDER'S Gear Locker

2006 Fender Jaguar MIJ Bass with Gig Bag. Sounds great with many tonal options and switches. Active electronics that sound great when recording. Some minor dings and scratches from playing live that are pictured. Original owner. No Trades

item.title: Jackson Spectra JS2 Bass Guitar- Snow White — $165
item.description:
$165 · Very Good · Ben's Shop

Active P/J pickups

24 frets and 34 inch scale

Sounds extremely good for the cost, lightweight, and suitable for every genre. Been played quite a bit but no noticeable wear.

item.title: Rickenbacker US Geddy Lee Artist Series Signature Jazz Bass - Stereo 2015 - black — $2,100
item.description:
$2,100 · Excellent · Gear Locker

🌟 Rickenbacker 4003S (2015 - Present) – Jetglo – Mint Condition with Dual/Stereo Outputs 🌟

Up for sale is a truly unique and stunning Rickenbacker 4003S in the timeless and sleek Jetglo finish. This instrument captures the legendary sustain, punchy treble, and deep low-end character that made Rickenbacker an absolute icon in rock history.

While the standard 4003S models only feature a mono jack, this specific bass comes equipped with Dual/Stereo outputs, giving you the full, legendary Rick-O-Sound capability to split your neck and bridge pickups into separate amplifiers or channels. Combined with the traditional, clean look of dot inlays and smooth, rounded body edges (no binding), this bass offers the ultimate blend of vintage aesthetic, modern playability, and premium tonal versatility.

It has been kept exclusively in a smoke-free, climate-controlled studio environment, meticulously cared for, and shows virtually zero signs of wear. No dings, no scratches, no buckle rash, and pristine frets—it looks and plays exactly like it just rolled off the line.

🔍 Key Features & Specs:

  • ⚫ Finish: Gloss Jetglo (Classic Black).
  • ⚫ Construction: Neck-thru-body construction for unparalleled, ringing sustain.
  • ⚫ Body & Neck Wood: Solid Maple body and maple neck with a beautiful Rosewood fingerboard.
  • ⚫ Neck Profile: Robust and stable, featuring the updated dual truss-rod system (2015-present spec) for perfect neck adjustments.
  • ⚫ Inlays: Traditional Dot inlays (S-version spec).
  • ⚫ Electronics: High-output single-coil pickups equipped with the Vintage Tone Circuit push-pull selector. Push it in for the modern 4003 punch, or pull it out to engage the vintage capacitor for that classic 4001 crispness and bite.
  • ⚫ Output: Premium Dual/Stereo outputs (Standard Mono + Rick-O-Sound capability).

📦 What’s Included:

  • 🔵 Original Rickenbacker Deluxe Hard-Shell Case (Mint condition).
  • 🔵 Original Case Candy, manual, and keys.

🛡️ Shipping & Shop Policy:

  • 🚀 Fast & Secure Shipping: Will be professionally packed, fully insured, and shipped with a tracking code immediately upon payment clearance.
  • ⚠️ Sold "As-Described": Please review all high-resolution photos carefully. This bass is exactly as pictured and described.

Don't miss out on adding a flawless, stereo-equipped Jetglo Ric to your collection. Feel free to message me with any questions!

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man Ball Family Reserve StingRay Special 4 HH — $2,000
item.description:
$2,000 · Very Good · Josh

Ernie Ball BFR HH stingray 4

item.title: Fender American Deluxe Jazz Bass with Rosewood Fretboard 2010 - 2016 - 3-Color Sunburst — $1,490
item.description:
$1,490 · Very Good · Joseph's Gear Outlet

2013 model

It plays and sounds great.

Weight is 9lbs

It comes with a soft gig bag.

Please feel free to ask any questions

Thank you!

item.title: 2001 Pedula PentaBuzz — $4,295
item.description:
$4,295 · Excellent · Emerald City Guitars

The Pedulla Pentabuzz has earned legendary status among fretless basses for its unmistakable character and effortless playability. Featuring a neck-through design, Bartolini electronics, and the signature coated ebony fingerboard, it delivers the expressive "mwah" that has made it a favorite of players like Mark Egan for decades. Whether you're chasing articulate jazz tones or modern fretless textures, the Pentabuzz remains one of the finest examples of Michael Pedulla's craftsmanship. Includes original hardshell case.

item.title: Cort Action PJ OPB V2 — $240.81
item.description:
$240.81 · Brand New · Music World Brilon

Cort Action PJ OPB V2 Open Pore Black

  • Korpus: Pappel
  • Decke: Pappel
  • Hals: Ahorn  
  • Griffbrett: Jatoba    
  • Mensur: 86,4 cm 
  • Griffbrettradius: 15,75'
  • 24 Bünde
  • Tonabnehmer: Powersound PSEB4-4/F & PSEB1-4/R
  • Regler: 2 x Volume, 1 x Tone  
  • Brücke: EB6(4)
  • Hardware: Chrome
  • Mechaniken: gekapselt
  • Farbe: Open Pore Black

item.title: FGN Neo Classic JB-100 — $1,576.28
item.description:
$1,576.28 · Brand New · Music World Brilon

FGN Neo Classic JB-100 - White Blonde

  • Korpus: Esche
  • Hals: Ahorn
  • Halsprofil: U Shape
  • Griffbrett: Ahorn
  • Mensur: 86,4 cm (34'')

  • Mechaniken: Gotoh Gb2
  • Hardware: Chrome und Nickel
  • Farbe: White Blonde
  • Finish: Gloss
  • inkl. FGN Gigbag

item.title: 2002 Pedula ThunderBass — $3,195
item.description:
$3,195 · Very Good · Emerald City Guitars

This lightly handled 2002 Pedula ThunderBass is an impeccably crafted instrument featuring neck-through construction, a figured maple top over a mahogany body, a fast maple neck with an ebony fingerboard, and Pedulla's proprietary Bartolini electronics with the Thunderguts switch for even more tonal range. This bass delivers a super-articulate, piano-like tone with a huge low-end response, and the playability is effortless thanks to the slim neck profile, which offers excellent upper-fret access. Since Pedulla ceased production in 2019, these American-made basses have become increasingly sought after, and it's easy to understand why the Thunderbass remains one of the finest boutique basses ever built. Includes original hardshell case.

item.title: Ibanez SR505-BM Standard Soundgear Bass 2004 - 2018 - Brown Mahogany — $399
item.description:
$399 · Very Good · Pilgrim Pete’s Gear Village

Great-playing Ibanez SR505-BM 5-string Soundgear bass, made in Korea in 2004. Finished in Brown Mahogany with a dark open-grain look. Slim neck, lightweight comfortable body, Bartolini MK1 pickups, and active EQ make it a versatile bass. Shows expected wear from age and use, but still looks good and plays well. New strings.

item.title: Reverend Thundergun Bass Metallic Silver Freeze with Gig Bag — $1,199
item.description:
$1,199 · Brand New · Truetone Music

The Thundergun is the bass of choice for thick old school tone, but with modern punch thanks to the five-piece neck and high mass bridge. The thicker raised center section increases body mass, while the thinner wings increase resonance for a wicked combination of sustain and harmonics.

Features Custom Pickups

We design our own proprietary custom pickups, to achieve the best tonal match with our instruments. Reverend pickups are also specifically designed for each position, for balanced volume and tone when switching.

Five-Piece Neck

Our bass necks are constructed from five pieces of wood laminated lengthwise. This creates a stronger and more stable neck that’s less prone to warping, twisting, and humps over the life of the instrument.

Korina Body

Korina (aka White Limba) is a medium-light weight wood, highly prized for consistency and tonal qualities. Often found in boutique and vintage instruments, all Reverends feature Korina bodies. A highly resonant wood, Korina is a key factor to producing our lively, responsive instruments that are rich in harmonics.

Thin Finish

The paint on a Reverend is thinner than most production guitars, allowing the body to vibrate more freely, for a more resonant and lively tone. Thick finishes might look a little 'slicker', but at Reverend, tone is a top priority. Our finish is also UV cured, which means it's 100% dry from day one, unlike vintage-style nitrocellulose finishes, which can take years to fully dry.

Hipshot Ultralight Tuners

These tuners have a housing made of aircraft grade aluminum that makes them extra-light. The difference in weight can be as much as 2-3oz less in the headstock, which means a more balanced bass that’s more comfortable to play. And the tone is great! Your bass is more defined and enhanced with the Hipshot Ultralites.

High Mass Bridge

Our bridge features heavy duty saddles in combination with a massive base plate. This substantial mass of metal enhances clarity and attack, and produces bell-like sustain. It's also a versatile bridge, that can be strung string-thru-body or top-load.

Initialed Headstock

Every Reverend is hand initialed and serial numbered on the back of the headstock by one of our highly skilled technicians who sets up, inspects, and tests every instrument.

Pickup Pan Control

This control allows you to blend the two pickups with one knob. Roll in the bridge pickup for more midrange punch, or bring up the neck pickup for more low thump and open highs. Dialing in the perfect tone has never been easier!

Custom Dome Knobs

Our proprietary knobs feature a lower profile which is less likely to be accidentally hit by the player's hand, and the white dot lets you easily see where your knob rotation is set.

Boneite Nut

We use "Boneite," a synthetic bone nut, that is more consistent than natural bone, on all Reverend instruments. It reduces friction, allowing the strings to slide through the nut slots smoothly, improving tuning stability.

Dual-Action Truss Rod

Our dual action rod adjusts in both directions, allowing back bow or forward bow. This assures correct adjustment range is possible regardless of string tension, extreme climate conditions, or the effects of long term aging. It’s also located at the headstock for easy access.

Custom Jack Plate

Our proprietary jack plate features four mounting screws, instead of two. The extra screws prevent the plate from tearing out of the body. Made of steel for durability, the laser etched logo adds an extra touch of class!

Pure Tone Jack

All Reverends use an upgraded jack by Pure Tone Technologies. The Pure Tone jack has dual contacts for improved grip, audio signal and reliability. The most dependable guitar jack available!

10% Pots

We use Alpha control pots with a +-10% rating, tighter and more consistent than a standard pot. Typically used in expensive boutique guitars, but standard issue in all Reverends.

10% Poly Caps

We use caps with a +-10% rating. Our caps are made to tighter specifications, so they’re more consistent. Made of polyester, they’re also longer lasting, and less affected by temperature than cheaper ceramic caps. Typically used in expensive boutique guitars.

SPECS Body: Korina Solidbody Pickups: Thick Brick Bridge, Split Brick Neck Bridge: String-thru-body or top-load, 3/4" spacing. Neck: 5-Piece Korina/Walnut Scale: 34" Neck Profile: Medium Oval Fingerboard: Rosewood - 12" Radius Frets: 21 - 0.110"W x 0.050"H Truss Rod: Dual Action, Headstock Access Tuners: Hipshot Ultralight, 1/2" Dia. Shaft Nut: 42mm width, Boneite Controls: Volume, Tone, Pickup Pan

SKU: TGUN-G-msvf-RW-TBSB-gmtl-HT

item.title: Epiphone Thunderbird IV 2014 with original hard case. Neck pickup is not working. — $357.53
item.description:
$357.53 · Fair · Fernando Nuti

For sale: Epiphone Thunderbird IV bass guitar with original hard case.

In excellent cosmetic condition.

The neck pickup is not working properly. It still produces a signal, but the output is extremely low.

The bridge pickup works perfectly, and the tone control functions perfectly as well.

Local pickup available in Italy, in the Treviso / Venice area.

The bass can be tested in Roncade, Treviso.

item.title: G2220 Electromatic Junior Jet Bass II Short-Scale, Shell Pink w/ Hardshell Case — $350
item.description:
$350 · Brand New · Barnett Music Exchange

Used: Very good condition - minor signs of wear, but in great playing condition.  Includes Gretsch hardshell case.

From the manufacturer: 

The Gretsch® Electromatic® Collection gives you essential Gretsch power, style and performance. These are the instruments that will get you to the big stage - Play Proud.

Sporting classic Gretsch appointments, the G2220 Electromatic® Junior Jet™ Bass II Short-Scale is easily capable of filling a room with massive subsonic tones.

Its key features include a 30.3"-shorter scale length for comfortable playability, basswood body, bolt-on maple neck, 12"-radius laurel fingerboard with 20 medium jumbo frets and dot inlays. A pair of Gretsch single-coil bass pickups deliver thundering sound, which can be commanded with a three-position pickup toggle switch, master volume and master tone.

This versatile bass is also equipped with a four-saddle adjustable bridge, synthetic bone nut and die-cast tuners.

  • Shorter 30.3" scale length
  • Basswood body
  • Bolt-on maple neck
  • 12"-radius laurel fingerboard with 20 medium jumbo frets and dot inlays
  • Gretsch single-coil bass pickups
  • Four-saddle adjustable bridge
  • Three-position pickup toggle switch, master volume and master tone

Body Basswood Body Back Basswood Body Binding None Body Finish Gloss Body Shape Jet™ Body Top Basswood Bridge 4-Saddle Adjustable Bridge Pickup Single-Coil Bass Color Shell Pink Commodity Code [object Object] Configuration SS Control Knobs G-Arrow Controls Master Volume, Master Tone Country Of Origin [object Object] Dimensions 5.00x15.00x46.50 IN Fingerboard Laurel Fingerboard Radius 12" (305 mm) Fret Size Medium Jumbo Hardware Finish Chrome Model Name G2220 Electromatic® Junior Jet™ Bass II Short-Scale, Shell Pink Neck Finish Gloss Neck Material Maple Neck Pickup Single-Coil Bass Number of Frets 20 Nut Material Synthetic Bone Nut Width 1.56" (39.62 mm) Orientation Right-Hand Pickguard Black Plexi Pickguard with White Gretsch® Logo Pickup Configuration SS Pickup Switching 3-Position Toggle: Position 1. Bridge Pickup, Position 2. Bridge And Neck Pickups, Position 3. Neck Pickup Position Inlays White Pearloid Dot Refinement Neck Material Maple Refinement Pickups Single-Coil Scale Length 30.3" (770 mm) Series Electromatic® String Nut Synthetic Bone Strings Nickel Plated Steel (.040-.095 Gauges) Tuning Machines Die-Cast

Thanks for shopping with BME!

item.description:

Peavey Foundation 4-String Red Electric Bass Guitar

item.title: 2011 Rickenbacker 4003 Midnight Blue — $3,100
item.description:
$3,100 · Excellent · Fanny’s House of Music

This classic Rickenbacker bass is as good as they get! Its signature Rick-o-Sound system outputs in both mono and stereo, the latter being accessible with this bass's included Rich-E-Split stereo splitter. It has a lovely, easy-to-play feel to its chunky D-shaped neck and a great, cutting bass sound—and it's in excellent condition!

Used/New/Vintage: Used

Repairs/Replacement Parts: Pickup cover removed and in case, Zero Mod thumbrest added, Rich-E-Split stereo splitter in case

Neck Shape: Chunky D-shape

Cosmetic Issues: Few, if any!

Finish: Midnight Blue

Serial Number: 11444411

Case: Original Hard Shell Case

Shipping costs vary by region. Items are returnable within 1 day of delivery. Items must be returned in original, as-shipped condition with all original packaging and no signs of use. Buyer assumes responsibility for all return shipping costs unless the item was not received as described.

item.title: Fender American Deluxe Jazz Bass 2014 - White — $1,095
item.description:
$1,095 · Very Good · Don’s Gear Locker

2014 Fender Deluxe Jazz Bass, White in very good condition. About 9.4 pounds. Everything works 100%. Strings are relatively new, new batteries installed. String through or top load bridge. 18 Volt Actives. White has started to mellow to a nice cream color. Sharp looking with the block inlays on the Rosewood Board. Very minor Fretwear, normal pickguard sctatches from regular use. These are hard to find in this color. Comes with Fender Hard case. No papers or tools. Packed and ready to go, so no other pictures are available. Listing is open to the US Lower 48 Only! NO INTERNATIONAL SHIPPING! Sorry.

item.title: De Gier BeBop 5 - Indian Yellow — $6,793.02
item.description:
$6,793.02 · Brand New · Doctorbass

De Gier BeBop 5 Indian Yellow Bass

Is this the "Holy Grail" of basses? We really think so.

If you've been in the market for years trying to find a perfect sounding Jazz Bass that really captures the essence of a true vintage instrument in terms of feel and sound but don't want any of the associated problems that come with an old instrument (neck and fret issues, sometimes electronics issue, huge dead spots, heavy weight, etc.) AND also need a 5 string bass, you will know this is a road that leads to frustration...until now!!!!

We can safely say this is the best 5 string Jazz Bass style instrument we've ever tried.

We've been aware of these amazing instruments handmade by Sander De Gier in The Netherlands for years but it wasn't until Guitar Summit 2025 in Mannheim that we got to personally test them... We were skeptical but as soon as we tried them we knew these were THE basses.

 

We can honestly say the the Bebop model is a 5 string Jazz Bass with a great low B (and 34") but that actually sounds like a vintage 4 string...

The neck feels incredibly comfortable under your hand thanks to the super nice profile.

A pair of handwound Lollar Single Coil pickups really capture the essence of a vintage 4 string Jazz Bass but with the added benefit of a great low B that sounds absolutely perfect and integrated with the rest of the strings.

The Bebop Classic bass is 100% passive but this is the Bebop model and the main difference is that it comes with an extremely interested and well designed active-passive preamp called Fatboost that is made by bass amp wizard Mark Vanderkley

Sure, there are a lot of nice JB style instruments in the market with Active-Passive electronics, but this is something else. Instead of using a preamp with a strong character and a big range of boost or cut, Sander De Gier has designed a simple preamp that actually only boosts the Lows slightly when engaged (the last pot is a two way Passive-Active rotary switch).

This means you get a 100% passive operation in that mode but also an incredibly well thought out active mode that sets this bass apart from any other in the industry.

Controls are Volume, Volume, Tone and 2 way active-passive switch. The passive tone works both in Passive and Active modes and it does exactly what you expect from it. You can remove some of the high end and also get that cool Jaco sound when you cut the Tone completely. Great.

Sander de Gier has been kind enough to explain what makes this active circuit different to just anything out there. Instead of going for a preamp with a huge range, he designed one with a slight bass boost as this is way more practical and musical than a big boost. It is enough to produce a slightly fuller sound and of course to create a perfect slap sound. Also there's no need to control the Midrange or the Treble because the mere fact of switching to Active mode will reveal a broader response in the High End because cable capacitance is not part of the sound any more. In passive mode the cable (and it's length and quality) will make a huge impact on the capacitance of the pickups so Treble response will be naturally cut. Some people think that in Passive mode you get to hear the true sound of the pickup but that's simply not true. The cable will always make an impact on the capacitance (the longer and pooer cable, the most treble frequencies are lost) but when you switch to Active mode, the cable disappears from the equation and it's then that you get to hear the true natural response of the pickups.

What's more, in Passive mode there's always a noticeable resonance peak from the pickups between 2 and 5kHz. The combination of this resonance peak and the roll off in treble response because of the cable's capacitance is what people tend to associate with passive instruments and passive sound.

When you switch to Active mode, you get a flatter Treble response and also the resonance peak is not as pronounced so you hear less mids and more treble. Combined with the bass boost of the preamp, this produces an incredibly musical and controlled "mid scoop" that's just amazing.

To sum up:

-Passive mode: excellent for vintage sounds, Rock, Pop, Jazz, Fingerstyle and pick

- Active mode: excellent for slap or more modern sounds but without ever being aggressive or too much. Simply perfect.

Add to all this a classic design and finish, perfect ergonomics and you have an absolutely beast of a bass that is a dream come true.

 

Some words by Sander de Gier himself: 

 

Bebop is the bass that put us on the map. I had been building boutique style basses (with gorgeous woods, laminates, preamps, etc) for many years. All the while customers asked: ‘when are you going to build a Jazz?’ After saying no for the 20th time or so, I finally thought, well why not? It changed everything. 

You may have heard the story that Marcus Miller bought the first Bebop and the next day Richard Bona ordered his. This is a true story, it happened just like that. After word got out, so many people ordered a Bebop, that it overtook everything else we were doing at the time. That was quickly followed by ‘are you going to do a 5 string as well? And a P?’. Bebop, and later on Soulmate, became the two models that De Gier is now known for.

Bebop is a modern bass. I took (obvious) inspiration from the classic Jazz bass, but did my own thing with it. I wanted to build a bass that was an all round modern work horse for the demanding player. 

Years of building basses had taught me how to build strong necks. Wood choice, build technique, glue choice, custom fret slots, small frets, compound radiuses fingerboard, dual action truss rod, carbon reinforced, thick headstock with light weight tuners, thick heel. There are many factors that determine the outcome here. 

For the bridge I choose brass, for its full range tonality and even balanced response. Bebop was supposed to be a passive bass, but I did add the Fatboost, a subtle bass boost to beef up the low end. 

These things amongst others make Bebop a modern iteration of a J style bass, very versatile, very comfortable, very cool.

If you need that pure vintage J tone, then Bebop Classic would be more your thing. If you want a bass that you can use all the time, then look no further. 

-Sander de Gier

item.title: Dingwall D-Roc Standard 4-String 2025 - Ducati White — $1,899
item.description:
$1,899 · Excellent · michael's Gear Emporium


This is an awesome Dingwall D-Roc in the beautiful Ducati white finish. It was manufactured by Dingwall in 2025. I bought it brand new a few months ago. It has only been used in my studio about 10 times. It has never been out of the studio since I bought it. It weighs in at just under 10lbs. It has a perfect setup from the Dingwall Canadian factory. The bass is a made by Hybrid Manufacturing: China / Canada. All the electronics work perfectly. The bass comes with a really nice Dingwall case, and comes with the case candy as well.

This bass comes from a Smoke-Free and Pet-Free studio environment. Contiguous USA buyers only ( I do not ship to Alaska or Hawaii). Thanks for viewing my listing, and have a Nice Day!!



The Dingwall D-Roc Standard is a player-driven instrument that sits on the rockier side of the tone tracks, though it is far from a one-trick pony!  With a 4-position D-Roc tuned Quad-Tone rotary pickup selector you have four distinct voicings.  The D-Roc Standard will help you deliver your own sound to your audience in its purest possible form. 

Dingwall Bag Included


Body & Neck:

The Dingwall D-Roc Body is constructed from an African Mahogany wood called Khaya, with a 5-piece Maple Neck and Pau Ferro fingerboard.  The bass is then finished with a polyurethane finish making the D-Roc really stand out. 


Pickups and Electronics:
The D-ROc Standard is triple-loaded with FD-3n passive pickups.  The key to their punchy tone is the use of Neodymium magnets; higher output means a more aggressive sound.  Using a D-Roc 4-Position Rotary Selector you can choose to operate just the bridge pickup, bridge and middle pickups, the middle pickup, or all three pickups!  Master Volume and Master Tone knobs are also featured. 


Hardware:
The Dingwall Minimalist Bridge features a custom riser system that's designed to prove an increased range of flexibility when it comes to adjusting the action.  If you play with a heavy technique, instead of raising your individual bridge saddles, which can put stress on the string and cause premature breakage, you can raise the entire bridge unit.  That way you can move between a high action or a low action just by adjusting the shims, which leaves the saddle adjustment only for fine tuning.  


Dingwall Strings:
Dingwall Bass Strings have been meticulously tested to maximize performance and deliver a tone that blends effortlessly with any style of music.  Dingwall was the first company to design strings specifically for multi-scale instruments.  Available in both Nickel and Stainless Steel, Dingwall Strings provide an even tension for optimal balance, letting you experience the warmest highs and the tightest lows.  Dingwall basses ship standard with Nickel Plated Steel strings. 


Dingwall D-Roc 4-String Specifications: 

  • Hybrid Manufacturing: China / Canada
  • Body Wood: Khaya (African Mahogany)
  • Neck Wood: 5-Piece Maple Bolt-On
  • Fingerboard Wood: Pau Ferro
  • Frets: 20 Banjo Sized
  • Radius: 9.45"
  • Scale Length: 34-36.25"
    • G = 34"
    • D = 34.75"
    • A = 35.5"
    • E = 36.25"
  • Pickups:  
    • 3 x Dingwall FD3n (Neodymium) 
  • Circuit: Passive
  • Controls: 
    • Master Volume
    • 4-Position Quad-Tone Pickup Selector
    • Master Tone
      • Bridge Solo
      • Bridge / Middle
      • Middle
      • Bridge / Middle / Neck
  • Bridge: Dingwall Minimalist 1-Piece Bridge
  • String Spacing @ Bridge: 19mm
  • Tuning Machines: Open Gear Y-Key
  • Nut: Graphtech Black Tusq
  • Case: Dingwall Padded Gigbag

item.title: Birdsong Corto2 Short Scale Custom Bass - 2018 - Cherry/Mahogany — $2,195
item.description:
$2,195 · Excellent · Luke's Guitar Shop

This is a beautiful 2018 Birdsong Corto 2 custom bass.  Cherry on Mahogany body with Maple neck, Rosewood fretboard, cherry headstock overlay, Rosewood truss rod cover and knobs, Ebony nut and cover plate and black hardware.  I see a couple minor marks on the top edge of the body but otherwise it is very clean and in excellent condition.  The frets are in excellent shape and it plays cleanly up and down the fretboard with good action and no buzzes or issues.  The pickups and controls function properly and it sounds great.  The weight is 7.13 lbs.  It comes in a TKL hardshell case and comes with the original COA/build sheet.  Please let me know if you have any questions.

item.title: Fender Limited Edition Suona Jazz Bass Thinline - Ebony Fingerboard Violin Burst — $2,450
item.description:
$2,450 · Excellent · O DiBella Music

Fender Limited Edition Suona Jazz Bass Thinline - Ebony Fingerboard Violin Burst


For your consideration is a USED Fender Limited Edition Suona Jazz Bass Thinline - Ebony Fingerboard Violin Burst

item.title: G&L L-2500 - USA 2007 - Left Handed - Sunburst w/OHSC — $1,895
item.description:
$1,895 · Mint · Luke's Guitar Shop

This is lefty handed 2007 American made G&L L2500 bass. Sunburst finish over an Alder body with maple neck and Rosewood fretboard. I see a bit of tarnish on the tuners (would easily polish off) but this bass has seen very little use and looks almost new.  The frets are in excellent shape and it plays cleanly up and down the neck with good action and no buzzes or issues. The pickups sound great and the controls work as they should. The weight on this one is 10.06 lbs. Bass is all original and it comes in with the original case and comes with the pictured paperwork. Please let me know if you have any questions. No trade offers please. Thank you!

• Production: USA

• Year: 2017

• body: ash

• Neck: maple

• fingerboard: maple

• Original Music Man electronics (active)

Condition: Very good, fully functional

Sound:

• Excellent attack, dense bottom, bright top

• Perfect for funk, rock, pop, fusion, live and studio.

Cosmetically, the instrument has some small scratches and light signs of use consistent with normal playing and age, but nothing that affects playability or functionality.

The bass sounds and plays great.

Comes with an original hard case 

Feel free to message me for additional photos or details.
Worldwide shipping available.

International Buyers - Please Note:

Import duties, taxes and charges are not included in the item price or shipping charges.

These charges are the buyer's responsibility.

Please check with your country's customs office to determine what these additional costs will be prior to bidding/buying.

These charges are normally collected by the delivering freight (shipping) company or when you pick the item up - do not confuse them for additional shipping charges.

item.title: Fender American Ultra Jazz Bass V with Maple Fretboard 2019 - 2020 - Plasma Red Burst — $2,400
item.description:
$2,400 · Excellent · Eduardo's Gear Garage

Bought brand new, had SweetWater set it up, has maybe 10 hrs of playing time. Gorgeous bass, just never gets used.

item.title: G&L L-2000 1992 USA w/case — $2,000
item.description:
$2,000 · Good · Bill's Gear Emporium

Bass has been gigged, so original poly finish shows some honest road wear and scratches. Onboard preamp was replaced with a new one a few years ago, but is still functional and will be included in the original hard shell case. Bass was pleked and re-fretted with vintage gauge fret wire two years ago at Weston Guitars in Ottawa. Slimmer neck, more like a Jazz Bass, with Indian rosewood fretboard. Bass weighs about 9 lbs. Original G&L Case is missing a couple of latches but still functional.

item.title: Fender Fullerton Precision Bass Ukulele 2023 - Present Olympic White — $300
item.description:
$300 · Excellent · Dillon's Gear Outlet

This tiny bass is so cool. When I came across this thing and played it, I knew I had to have one. I like it as a simple practice/travel tool, but plugging it is wild. There's so much tone that comes out of this thing.

The problem I have is simply that I have too many bass guitars to play this like I should. Maybe you could be the next owner and play it like it needs to be played? I mean, it’s practically brand new (cue the Sarah McLachlan music) and in need of someone to take it out of the box.

Please let me know if you have any questions. If you've made it this far, it's probably a safe bet that you know what you're looking at. And in all seriousness, this thing is fantastic. I’m here to answer any questions.

-Dillon
